comments.text: We speht a week here in 2017...we weren't impressed....the cities and towns have been neglected since the brits moved out in 1972. Bo9ze and restaurants are expensive.  We stayed in the Corinthia Palace hotel in Attard,supposedly 5 star...it was anything but. Plus we didnt see one cat

comments.author: @GlobalLifeDiscovery
comments.text: It’s haunting how a place so sun-drenched and beautiful can harbor such deep, chilling secrets underground. This video truly makes you look at Malta through a completely different lens.

comments.author: @DISGlobalUS
comments.text: What surprised you most about Malta: its deep religious traditions, its fascinating history, or the hidden places beneath the island?

comments.author: @GlobeLifeDocs
comments.text: Pro tip for anyone visiting: Skip the crowded summer peaks and come in October. The sea is still warm from the summer heat, but the Gozo ferries are empty and you can actually hear yourself think in Mdina! ✈

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Thank you for sharing it! October really sounds like the perfect balance — warm sea, quieter streets, and a more peaceful side of Malta that many visitors probably miss in the summer rush. Mdina must feel even more magical when it’s calm like that.

replies.author: @GlobalLifeDiscovery
replies.text: Thanks for the advice. Empty ferries and warm water sound much better than the summer crowds. I will keep that in mind.

comments.author: @RealWorldStories-p5c
comments.text: Paradise is most seductive when it hides a hell beneath; beauty never tells the whole truth

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Malta really does have that contrast, doesn’t it? Bright blue seas, golden streets, deep faith, and then these silent underground mysteries that make you wonder what the island is still hiding. Sometimes the most beautiful places are the ones with the heaviest stories beneath them. What part of Malta’s hidden side surprised you the most?

comments.author: @GlobalCountryExplorer
comments.text: This isn't just a sunny resort island; it’s a living fortress of secrets. Watching their elegant, devout women navigate such a heavy, dark underground heritage leaves this old man stunned.

replies.author: @GlobalLifeDiscovery
replies.text: The contrast between the sunny island and that dark history is wild. Seeing those devout women navigate it was very moving.

comments.author: @RealLifeFilesDoc
comments.text: When the video begins, it’s easy to carry the illusion of a sun-drenched Malta, gorgeous women, or a superficial pious faith as the title suggests. But as the narrative is peeled back throughout this 12-minute journey, the documentary strips bare a completely different hidden corner deep underground. By the time the final frame of the ancient ruins fades out, that comfortable distance is completely shattered. You're left with a profound realization that this isn't just a magnificent tourist island—it's a historical witness concealing chilling secrets, shattering the flawless picture the outside world still believes in.

replies.author: @GlobalLifeDiscovery
replies.text: You said it perfectly. That 12 minute journey really showed a side of Malta I never expected. It is not just for tourists.

comments.author: @勝則平川
comments.text:
創世記が唯一の神・信仰の起源を　説明したのに・対し　出エジプト記は　次の・宗祖ダビデを語っている　故に　十戒は・ダビデの・十戒です
ダビデの・根拠は　出エジプト記１７－８アマレクとの戦いです　アマレクとの戦いは　　サムエル記３０アマレク人に対するダビデの出撃
サムエル記３０－８ダビデは主に託宣を求めた　　ダビデが神に託宣を求め・戦った　戦争・故に　語られている

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Malta also carries a long spiritual history, so your perspective adds another interesting layer to the conversation. What part of Malta’s religious or ancient history interested you the most?

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Malta truly is a small island with a very big story. Its culture, history, and hidden mysteries make it such a fascinating place to explore. Have you ever visited Malta, or is it a country you’d like to see one day?
